title: Tapping for the Event I Can't Release
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the thing still being carried
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the event or person you have not yet forgiven. The script addresses the stuck point directly: the belief that forgiving means excusing, and the permission to put down the weight on your own timeline.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are carrying something you know is costing you and you cannot yet let it go. Good when forgiveness feels like losing, or when you have been told to move on and the instruction has not helped.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the event or person that is still with you. The script addresses the stuck point in forgiveness: the belief that letting go means excusing what happened, and the permission to move toward release on your own timeline.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I am still carrying this and I want to put it down, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I know holding this is costing me and I cannot let it go yet, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am not ready to forgive and I know I cannot rush it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I am still carrying this event.
**Side of eye:** It comes back when I least expect it.
**Under eye:** The anger is still here. Or the hurt. Or both.
**Under nose:** I want to put this down and I cannot.
**Chin:** Part of me does not want to forgive. It feels like losing.
**Collarbone:** Forgiving feels like saying it was okay, and it was not okay.
**Under arm:** I am allowed to take my time with this.
**Top of head:** The event is still with me and I have not finished with it yet.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I do not have to forgive on a schedule.
**Side of eye:** Forgiveness is a process, not a decision made once.
**Under eye:** I can release the weight of this without excusing it.
**Under nose:** Letting go of the event does not mean it was fine.
**Chin:** I can free myself without freeing them from accountability.
**Collarbone:** Forgiveness is something I do for me, not for them.
**Under arm:** I can move toward releasing this in my own time.
**Top of head:** I do not have to carry this forever. I get to choose when I put it down.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
